input.title: RecursionError
input.error_message:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "main.py", line 4, in <module>
    calculate_0()
  File "main.py", line 2, in calculate_0
    return calculate_0()
RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded

output.technical: A `RecursionError` is raised because the function calls itself infinitely without a base case, hitting Python's maximum recursion depth.
output.beginner: Your function is stuck in an endless loop calling itself.
output.analogy: Like looking into two mirrors facing each other, it never ends.
output.solution: Add a base case to your recursive function to tell it when to stop.
output.prevention: Ensure all recursive functions have a condition that will eventually be met to stop the recursion.
